docs.title: About Sovereign Codex
docs.text:
Welcome to the Sovereign Codex.

Sovereign Codex is an ongoing community knowledge base for Black Desert Online, designed to provide structured, searchable, and continuously maintained documentation.

The project was created with a simple goal:

Build a trusted source of information that is easy to navigate, easy to reference, and continually improved over time.

Unlike a traditional wiki, Sovereign Codex focuses on carefully organised and verified documentation. Content is developed using documented standards and governance to ensure consistency as the project grows.

Whether you're looking for information on ocean content, game systems, progression, or future additions, the aim is to make finding reliable information as straightforward as possible.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
The documentation is organised into several key areas:

  • About — Information about the project and its purpose.
  • Governance — How the project is managed and maintained.
  • Standards — Documentation standards and writing guidelines.
  • Knowledge — Verified documentation and technical guides.
  • Research — Information currently being investigated or validated.
  • Roadmap — Development plans, milestones, and upcoming features.

docs.title: An Ongoing Project
docs.text:
Sovereign Codex is continuously evolving.

As Black Desert Online changes, the documentation will continue to grow, improve, and be refined over time.

Feedback, corrections, and contributions are always welcome.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
Welcome to the About section of the Sovereign Codex.

This section introduces the project, its purpose, and the principles that guide its development. Whether you're visiting the Codex for the first time or looking to understand how the project is managed, these pages provide an overview of what Sovereign Codex is and what it aims to achieve.

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• Project Philosophy — The core beliefs and guiding principles behind the Codex.
  • Project Vision — The long-term goals and direction of the project.
  • Changelog — A history of significant project milestones and releases.

If you're new to Sovereign Codex, this is the best place to start.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
Sovereign Codex Version 1.0.0 Foundation Release

✓ Information Architecture Finalised ✓ Governance Framework Established ✓ Documentation Standards Published ✓ Repository Structure Locked ✓ Navigation Architecture Complete ✓ MkDocs Site Operational ✓ Foundation Freeze Enacted

The Sovereign Codex has transitioned from planning to active development.

Future releases will focus on expanding knowledge and improving user experience rather than restructuring the platform.

docs.title: Project Philosophy
docs.text:
Sovereign Codex exists to make reliable information easier to find, easier to understand, and easier to maintain.

The project was founded on the belief that good documentation is more than simply collecting information. Knowledge should be organised, verified, and presented in a way that allows people to quickly find answers while understanding the context behind them.

Rather than creating another collection of guides, Sovereign Codex aims to become a structured knowledge base that can evolve alongside Black Desert Online.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text: The project is guided by a small number of fundamental principles.

docs.title: Accuracy over speed
docs.text:
Information should be verified before it is published.

Providing reliable documentation is more valuable than publishing information first.

docs.title: Structure over volume
docs.text:
Well-organised documentation is more useful than a large collection of disconnected pages.

Every page should have a clear purpose and a logical place within the knowledge base.

docs.title: Clarity over complexity
docs.text:
Technical subjects should be explained as simply as possible without sacrificing accuracy.

Documentation should be approachable for new players while remaining valuable to experienced players.

docs.title: Continuous improvement
docs.text:
Documentation is never considered "finished."

As new information becomes available or game systems change, the Codex should continue to evolve.

docs.title: Community driven
docs.text: While the Codex is curated to maintain consistency and quality, community feedback, corrections, and discussion are an important part of keeping the knowledge base accurate and relevant.

docs.title: Our Goal
docs.text:
The objective of Sovereign Codex is not to document everything.

The objective is to document things well.

Every page should contribute towards building a trusted reference that players can confidently return to whenever they need reliable information.

docs.title: Project Vision
docs.text:
Sovereign Codex is intended to become a comprehensive and trusted knowledge base for Black Desert Online.

The long-term vision is to create documentation that is organised, searchable, easy to navigate, and maintained to a consistent standard, allowing players to quickly find reliable information without searching through multiple guides, videos, or Discord discussions.

As the project evolves, the Codex will continue to expand while maintaining the principles of accuracy, clarity, and consistency established by its governance and documentation standards.

docs.title: Build a trusted reference
docs.text: Develop documentation that players can confidently rely on for accurate and well-presented information.

docs.title: Improve accessibility
docs.text: Present complex game systems in a structured way that makes them easier to understand for both new and experienced players.

docs.title: Support continuous improvement
docs.text: Treat the Codex as a living project that evolves alongside Black Desert Online through ongoing research, verification, and refinement.

docs.title: Encourage community participation
docs.text: Welcome constructive feedback, corrections, and ideas that help improve the quality and accuracy of the knowledge base.

docs.title: Establish a sustainable platform
docs.text: Create documentation that is designed to remain maintainable over the long term through consistent governance, standards, and information architecture.

docs.title: Looking Ahead
docs.text:
While the initial focus is on Ocean content, the architecture has been designed to support a much broader knowledge base over time.

Future areas may include additional game systems, progression guides, life skills, equipment, mechanics, and other verified technical documentation as the project continues to grow.

The vision is not simply to build a collection of guides, but to establish a long-term resource that the community can rely upon for years to come.

docs.title: Governance Architecture
docs.text:
The Governance Architecture defines the structural foundation of Sovereign Codex.

Its purpose is to establish a consistent information architecture that supports long-term growth while maintaining clarity, organisation, and maintainability.

Rather than being redesigned as the project expands, the architecture provides a stable framework upon which new documentation can be added.

docs.title: Information Architecture
docs.text:
Sovereign Codex is organised into six primary sections.

Section Purpose About Introduces the project, its purpose, and its development. Governance Defines how the project is managed and maintained. Standards Establishes documentation standards and writing conventions. Knowledge Contains verified documentation and technical guides. Research Stores information undergoing investigation or verification. Roadmap Documents project milestones, planned improvements, and future development.

Each section has a clearly defined responsibility to minimise overlap and maintain a logical separation of concerns.

docs.title: Documentation Lifecycle
docs.text:
Information progresses through the project using a structured workflow.

Research → Review → Knowledge

Information is researched, verified, reviewed, and ultimately published within the Knowledge section once it meets the project's documentation standards.

docs.title: Structural Stability
docs.text:
The information architecture is intended to remain stable over time.

As the project grows, new documentation should generally be added within the existing structure rather than introducing new top-level categories.

This approach promotes consistency, simplifies navigation, and reduces long-term maintenance.

docs.title: Foundation Freeze
docs.text:
Version 1.0.0 establishes the initial architectural foundation of Sovereign Codex.

The following elements are considered foundational:

  • Information architecture
  • Folder hierarchy
  • Navigation hierarchy
  • Naming conventions
  • Governance framework
  • Documentation standards

Changes to these foundational elements should be deliberate, documented, and supported by a clear justification.

docs.title: Future Development
docs.text:
Future improvements should prioritise expanding the knowledge base and improving usability while preserving the integrity of the established architecture.

The objective is to evolve the content without continually restructuring the platform.

docs.title: Evidence Model
docs.text:
The Evidence Model defines how information is evaluated before it is published within Sovereign Codex.

Its purpose is to promote accuracy, transparency, and consistency by establishing clear expectations for the quality and reliability of documented information.

Rather than requiring absolute certainty, the Evidence Model encourages documenting information with an appropriate level of confidence while clearly identifying assumptions and limitations where necessary.

docs.title: Evidence Sources
docs.text: Information published within Sovereign Codex may be supported by one or more of the following evidence sources.

docs.title: In-Game Observation
docs.text:
Information directly observed and verified within Black Desert Online.

Examples include:

  • User interface behaviour
  • NPC interactions
  • Item descriptions
  • System mechanics
  • Gameplay testing

docs.title: Official Sources
docs.text:
Information published by Pearl Abyss through official channels.

Examples include:

  • Patch notes
  • Official announcements
  • Event information
  • Developer communications

docs.title: Community Research
docs.text:
Information produced through testing, experimentation, or collaboration within the player community.

Community research should be independently verified where practical before being presented as established knowledge.

docs.title: Historical Information
docs.text:
Documentation describing previous game systems, mechanics, or historical changes.

Historical information should clearly distinguish between current and legacy content.

docs.title: Confidence
docs.text:
Not all information carries the same level of certainty.

Where appropriate, documentation should distinguish between:

  • Verified
  • Supported
  • Preliminary
  • Speculative

Readers should be able to understand the confidence associated with published information.

docs.title: Assumptions
docs.text:
Where conclusions rely upon assumptions, those assumptions should be documented.

Assumptions should never be presented as established fact.

docs.title: Corrections
docs.text:
New evidence should take precedence over older information.

When reliable evidence contradicts existing documentation, the documentation should be reviewed and updated accordingly.

Maintaining accuracy is more important than preserving previous conclusions.

docs.title: Governance Principles
docs.text:
The Governance Principles establish the fundamental values that guide the development and maintenance of Sovereign Codex.

Every governance decision, documentation standard, and editorial process should support these principles.

docs.title: Accuracy
docs.text:
Information should be as accurate as reasonably possible at the time it is published.

Where uncertainty exists, it should be clearly identified rather than omitted or presented as fact.

docs.title: Consistency
docs.text:
Documentation should follow consistent structures, terminology, and presentation.

Consistency improves readability, reduces ambiguity, and makes the knowledge base easier to maintain over time.

docs.title: Transparency
docs.text:
Editorial decisions, assumptions, and evidence should be documented wherever appropriate.

Readers should be able to understand how conclusions were reached and what information supports them.

docs.title: Maintainability
docs.text:
Documentation should be written with long-term maintenance in mind.

Clear organisation, reusable standards, and structured information reduce the effort required to keep documentation current as the game evolves.

docs.title: Accessibility
docs.text:
Information should be presented in a way that is approachable for both new and experienced players.

Complex subjects should be explained clearly without unnecessarily increasing technical complexity.

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
Constructive feedback and contributions are encouraged.

While Sovereign Codex is curated to maintain quality and consistency, community involvement plays an important role in identifying improvements and expanding the knowledge base.

docs.title: Continuous Improvement
docs.text:
Documentation is never considered complete.

As Black Desert Online evolves, Sovereign Codex should continue to improve through research, verification, review, and refinement.

docs.title: Review Process
docs.text:
The Review Process defines the workflow used to evaluate, refine, and publish documentation within Sovereign Codex.

Its purpose is to ensure that information is reviewed for accuracy, consistency, and quality before it becomes part of the project's verified knowledge base.

The process is intended to support continuous improvement while maintaining the integrity of published documentation.

docs.title: Documentation Workflow
docs.text:
Documentation generally progresses through the following stages:

  1. Research
  2. Draft
  3. Review
  4. Approval
  5. Publication
  6. Ongoing Maintenance

Each stage contributes to improving the quality and reliability of the final documentation.

docs.title: Research
docs.text:
Information is gathered from available evidence sources, including in-game observation, official communications, and verified community research.

Where uncertainty exists, it should be identified during this stage.

docs.title: Draft
docs.text:
Information is organised into a structured document that follows the project's documentation standards.

Drafts should focus on clarity, completeness, and logical organisation.

docs.title: Review
docs.text:
Documentation is reviewed for:

  • Technical accuracy
  • Consistency with project standards
  • Supporting evidence
  • Readability
  • Internal links and references

Where necessary, revisions are made before publication.

docs.title: Approval
docs.text:
Once the documentation satisfies the project's governance and documentation standards, it may be approved for publication.

Approval indicates that the document represents the best available information at the time of publication.

docs.title: Publication
docs.text:
Approved documentation becomes part of the Knowledge section of Sovereign Codex.

Published documentation should remain accessible, maintainable, and appropriately linked to related content.

docs.title: Ongoing Maintenance
docs.text:
Publication is not considered the final stage of the documentation lifecycle.

As Black Desert Online evolves, documentation should be reviewed and updated to reflect new evidence, gameplay changes, or improvements in understanding.

Continuous maintenance is an essential part of maintaining a trusted knowledge base.

docs.title: Governance Vision
docs.text:
The Governance framework is intended to provide a stable foundation that enables Sovereign Codex to grow without sacrificing quality, consistency, or maintainability.

As the project evolves, governance should continue to support the development of trusted documentation while remaining adaptable to new technologies, workflows, and community contributions.

Governance is not intended to restrict progress. Its purpose is to provide a consistent framework that allows the project to evolve with confidence.

docs.title: Preserve Quality
docs.text: Maintain documentation standards that promote accuracy, clarity, and consistency across all published content.

docs.title: Support Sustainable Growth
docs.text: Ensure the governance framework remains scalable as the knowledge base expands into new subject areas and increases in complexity.

docs.title: Encourage Transparency
docs.text:
Document governance decisions, architectural changes, and editorial processes wherever practical.

Transparency promotes trust, improves maintainability, and provides context for future contributors.

docs.title: Enable Continuous Improvement
docs.text:
Review and refine governance processes as the project matures.

Changes should be made deliberately and supported by practical experience rather than unnecessary complexity.

docs.title: Support Community Contributions
docs.text:
Encourage constructive feedback and discussion while maintaining clear editorial standards and project consistency.

Community participation should strengthen the quality of the knowledge base without compromising its governance principles.

docs.title: Looking Forward
docs.text:
Governance should remain stable, predictable, and easy to understand.

As Sovereign Codex continues to grow, the framework should support new ideas without requiring continual redesign of the project's foundations.

The long-term vision is to establish a governance model that encourages sustainable development while preserving the quality and integrity of the knowledge base for years to come.

docs.title: Standards
docs.text:
The Standards section defines the conventions used when creating and maintaining documentation within Sovereign Codex.

These standards promote consistency across the project by establishing common approaches to writing, formatting, organising, and maintaining documentation.

Unlike Governance, which defines the principles and processes of the project, the Standards describe how documentation should be produced in practice.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The Standards establish guidance for:

  • Documentation structure
  • Naming conventions
  • Writing style
  • Markdown formatting
  • Evidence and citations
  • Page templates
  • Versioning practices

Together, these standards help ensure that documentation remains clear, consistent, and maintainable as the project grows.

docs.title: Standards
docs.text:
• Project Standards — General documentation expectations.
  • Naming Conventions — Naming rules for pages, folders, and headings.
  • Markdown Style — Formatting and writing conventions.
  • Evidence & Citations — Referencing and supporting information.
  • Templates — Standard page structures.
  • Versioning — Documentation version management.

docs.title: Project Standards
docs.text:
The Project Standards establish the general expectations for all documentation published within Sovereign Codex.

These standards provide a consistent foundation for creating documentation that is accurate, readable, maintainable, and aligned with the project's governance framework.

All documentation should be developed with the reader in mind while maintaining consistency across the knowledge base.

docs.title: General Principles
docs.text:
Documentation should be:

  • Accurate
  • Clear
  • Consistent
  • Maintainable
  • Appropriately evidenced

These principles apply to all documentation regardless of subject matter.

docs.title: Writing Style
docs.text:
Documentation should be written using clear, concise language.

Where technical terminology is necessary, it should be explained or supported with sufficient context for readers unfamiliar with the subject.

Avoid unnecessary complexity where simpler explanations achieve the same result.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
Each document should have:

  • A clear purpose
  • Logical headings
  • Consistent formatting
  • Appropriate cross references
  • Supporting evidence where required

Information should be organised so readers can quickly locate the content they need.

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text:
Documentation should be reviewed periodically to ensure it remains accurate and relevant.

Outdated information should be updated or removed as appropriate.

The objective is to maintain documentation that reflects the best available understanding at the time of publication.

docs.title: Continuous Improvement
docs.text:
Project Standards should evolve as Sovereign Codex matures.

Changes to documentation standards should be deliberate, documented, and intended to improve the consistency, quality, or maintainability of the project.
